domain: chronobiology (zeitgeber entrainment and oscillator phase locking)
specific_mechanism: An internal circadian oscillator entrains (phase-locks) to external zeitgebers (light, temperature, feeding); the entrainment depends on zeitgeber strength × phase response curve; multiple zeitgebers can act simultaneously and reinforce or conflict; phase-locking is bounded — outside an entrainment range the internal oscillator drifts free
llm_application: An agent runtime where periodic background tasks are phase-locked to externally provided zeitgebers (user-activity cycles, batch boundaries, scheduled checkpoints); if zeitgebers conflict or fall outside entrainment range, the agent's background tasks (memory consolidation, cache cleanup, batch sync) drift free, providing a diagnostic signature of zeitgeber-environment misalignment.
novelty_claim: Existing agent runtimes use fixed-interval or event-driven background tasks. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work formalizes a chronobiology-style oscillator-phase-locked runtime where free drift outside entrainment is itself a diagnostic.
